channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Biden admin appeals plea agreements with Guantánamo detainees, including 9/11 mastermind
item.description: The Biden administration is asking for a federal appeals court to temporarily block a plea deal agreement with three detainees in Guantanamo Bay, Cuba.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Disgraced former US Rep. George Santos seeks to delay fraud sentencing to make more podcast episodes
item.description: Disgraced former congressman George Santos has asked a judge to delay his sentencing on federal fraud charges until the summer so he can pay off more than $500,000 in fines with revenue from a new podcast.
item.dc:creator: Philip Marcelo, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: 5 takeaways from Trump's Mar-a-Lago press conference
item.description: Trump's remarks were typically wide-ranging and encompassed topics from conflicts in the Middle East and Ukraine to Trump’s long-standing grievances.
item.dc:creator: Niall Stanage, NewsNation

item.title: Takeaways from Trump’s Mar-a-Lago press conference
item.description: In a press conference at his Mar-a-Lago club in Florida, Trump would not rule out using military force to seize control of the Panama Canal and Greenland, citing national security interests.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: RFK Jr. to meet with slew of Dems including Elizabeth Warren, Bernie Sanders
item.description: RFK Jr. begins meeting with Senate Democrats this week as his coalition for getting confirmed to HHS secretary remains uncertain.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: These Democrats flipped their votes on the Laken Riley Act
item.description: Seven House Democrats flipped their votes to "yes" on a GOP-led bill named after the slain Georgia student Laken Riley that would require detention of migrants arrested for theft.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: US says Sudanese rebel force has committed genocide and imposes sanctions on the group's leaders
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Biden administration said Tuesday that a Sudanese paramilitary group and its proxies are committing genocide in a civil war with the country's military that has killed tens of thousands of people, leveling sanctions on the group’s leader and affiliated companies.
item.dc:creator: MATTHEW LEE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Exxon sues California AG for defamation after he sued them for 'misleading' plastic recycling claims
item.description: A major oil company has responded to California Attorney General Rob Bonta with a lawsuit alleging defamation after he accused them of misleading customers.
item.dc:creator: Cameron Kiszla, Fox 5

item.title: Curator talks archiving Walz’s bid for VP, preserving political legacies
item.description: While brief, Gov. Tim Walz’s bid for vice president will add another chapter to his legacy in politics, and the Minnesota Historical Society has gotten a jumpstart on preserving it.
item.dc:creator: Cathy Wurzer and Gracie Stockton, MPR News

item.title: Danish prime minister has blunt message for Trump: Greenland is not for sale
item.description: Denmark's Prime Minister Mette Frederiksen emphasized that Greenland is not for sale as President-elect Trump insists the U.S. should acquire the territory.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Harris will travel to Asia, Mideast and Europe during her final week in office
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Vice President Kamala Harris plans to close out her term with an around-the-world trip making stops in Singapore, Bahrain and Germany, her office said. The trip, which is scheduled to last from Jan. 13 to Jan. 17, will be a final opportunity for Harris to address U.S.
item.dc:creator: CHRIS MEGERIAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump's praise of Carter in death after jeering him in life deepens a contradictory relationship
item.description: PALM BEACH, Fla. (AP) — Living to 100 let Jimmy Carter fulfill his wish to vote for Democratic Vice President Kamala Harris against Republican Donald Trump in November. His death means flags at the White House will be at half-staff when Trump regains the presidency on Jan. 20.
item.dc:creator: WILL WEISSERT and MICHELLE L. PRICE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: House Republican's bill would rip federal funds from states that give illegal immigrants driver's licenses
item.description: Rep. Claudia Tenney put pressure on New York Gov. Kathy Hochul to repeal the Green Light Law with a bill threatening to pull federal highway funds.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump won't commit to not using military to get Panama Canal
item.description: “Look, the Panama Canal is vital to our country, it’s being operated by China, China. And we gave the Panama Canal to Panama, we didn’t give it to China,” Trump said.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, NewsNation

item.title: Speaker Johnson: ‘Intention’ is to increase debt limit in massive Trump agenda bill
item.description: Speaker Mike Johnson (R-La.) said congressional Republicans plan to increase the nation’s borrowing limit as a part of a massive party-line bill they are crafting that will encapsulate President-elect Trump’s legislative agenda.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump refuses to rule out use of military force to take control of Greenland and the Panama Canal
item.description: President-elect Donald Trump says he won't rule out the use of military force to seize control of the Panama Canal and Greenland as he declared U.S. control of both to be vital to American national security.
item.dc:creator: Will Weissert And Zeke Miller, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Elon Musk helped Trump win. Now he's looking at Europe, and many politicians are alarmed
item.description: Fresh from pouring his money and energies into helping Donald Trump win reelection, Elon Musk has trained his sights on Europe.
item.dc:creator: Jill Lawless, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Judge temporarily blocks release of special counsel report on Trump cases as court fight simmers
item.description: A federal judge in Florida has temporarily blocked the release of special counsel Jack Smith’s report on investigations into Donald Trump, who says it would be a “fake report” anyway.
item.dc:creator: Eric Tucker, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: New California law aims to prevent book bans in libraries statewide
item.description: By 2026, libraries in San Diego must come up with a collection development policy that would outline how they choose what books they'll add to their shelves, as well as provide a way for readers to express their concerns about the library materials and giving them a way to request new ones.
item.dc:creator: Dominique LaVigne, Fox 5

item.title: Saudi Arabia presses Florida man to give up US citizenship over critical tweets, family says
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Saudi Arabia has forced a Florida retiree to try to renounce his American citizenship after jailing him over social media posts critical of the kingdom’s crown prince, according to the man’s son.
item.dc:creator: JOSHUA GOODMAN and ELLEN KNICKMEYER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Biden tells New Orleans mourners they are not alone as he honors victims of attack
item.description: NEW ORLEANS (AP) — President Joe Biden told mourners in New Orleans on Monday that they are not alone as he paid tribute to victims of the deadly New Year’s attack and channeled the pain felt by their loved ones. Biden made the remarks at St. Louis Cathedral in the city's historic French Quarter.
item.dc:creator: COLLEEN LONG and MICHELLE L. PRICE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Minnesota Office of Cannabis Management will get new interim director
item.description: The interim director of the Minnesota Office of Cannabis Management, who has led the office since it was created, is stepping down.
item.dc:creator: MPR News Staff, MPR News

item.title: Canada's Trudeau announces resignation after nearly a decade as prime minister
item.description: TORONTO (AP) — Canadian Prime Minister Justin Trudeau announced his resignation Monday after nearly a decade in power, bowing to rising discontent over his leadership and growing turmoil within his government signaled by the abrupt departure of his finance minister.
item.dc:creator: ROB GILLIES, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Trump pitches 'merged' US, Canada after Trudeau resignation announcement
item.description: After Trudeau’s announcement Monday, Trump touted the idea that many Canadians would “LOVE” the idea of being the "51st State" of the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, NewsNation

item.title: Judge holds Giuliani in contempt in Georgia election workers case
item.description: A federal judge held Rudy Giuliani in civil contempt on Monday for failing to comply with court orders in two former Georgia election workers’ efforts to collect their $146 million defamation judgment. At the end of a two-day hearing that saw Giuliani take the stand, U.S.
item.dc:creator: Zach Schonfeld, Fox 5

item.title: Trump pitches 'merged' US, Canada after Trudeau resignation announcement
item.description: After Trudeau’s announcement Monday, Trump touted the idea that many Canadians would “LOVE” the idea of being the "51st State" of the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, Fox 5

item.title: Pence: 'Admirable' that Harris certified Trump's win after losing election
item.description: Former Vice President Mike Pence, who had to be evacuated four years ago Monday as rioters stormed the Capitol, welcomed "the return of order and civility" as Congress moved to certify President-elect Donald Trump's 2024 victory.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, Fox 5

item.title: GOP sues over timing of special election crucial to Minnesota House power balance
item.description: A lawsuit challenges a decision by Gov. Tim Walz to schedule a Jan. 28 special election after a November winner was deemed ineligible to take office in the House on Jan. 14.
item.dc:creator: Clay Masters, MPR News

item.title: Child tax credit expansion proposed: What it means for New Yorkers
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) – A proposal to expand New York's child tax credit would give families more money per child, Gov. Kathy Hochul announced on Monday.
item.dc:creator: Erin Pflaumer, PIX 11

item.title: Live updates: Congress certifies Trump's election victory
item.description: President-elect Trump's election victory will be certified on Monday, marking a sense of vindication for his team and a blow to his critics, while Kamala Harris is preparing to affirm his victory and Elon Musk extends his political influence over Europe.
item.dc:creator: The Hill staff, NewsNation

item.title: Trump lawyers push to halt Jan. 10 hush money case sentencing
item.description: President-elect Trump’s legal team urged a judge to hold off on sentencing him on Jan. 10 on his New York criminal conviction ahead of his inauguration.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, Fox 5

item.title: President-elect Trump's lawyers ask judge to halt sentencing in hush money case
item.description: Editor's note: The video above is from Jan. 3, 2025. NEW YORK (AP) — President-elect Donald Trump asked a judge Monday to halt this week’s sentencing in his hush money case while they appeal a ruling upholding the verdict.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Wisconsin’s Evers revives plan to enable voters to repeal and create state laws
item.description: Wisconsin Gov. Tony Evers is reviving his plan to enable voters to repeal and create state laws, this time through the state budget.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Harris readies to certify Trump’s election win – and her decisive defeat
item.description: Kamala Harris is readying on Monday to certify the 2024 election results, affirming President-elect Trump’s victory as well as her decisive defeat to him after a whirlwind run for commander in chief.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, NewsNation

item.title: Melania Trump to debut documentary film on Amazon in 2025
item.description: “As a private person, who has often been the subject of public scrutiny and misrepresentation, I feel a responsibility to clarify the facts,” First Lady Melania Trump said.
item.dc:creator: Tanya Noury, NewsNation

item.title: Legacy of Jan. 6 prosecutions in flux with Trump presidency on horizon
item.description: The Justice Department’s prosecution of the Jan. 6, 2021, Capitol attack — hailed as one of the “largest, most complex, and most resource-intensive investigations” in its history — could soon go out with a fizzle.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, NewsNation

item.title: Judge sets Trump's sentencing in hush money case for Jan. 10, but signals no jail time
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — In an extraordinary turn, a judge Friday set President-elect Donald Trump's sentencing in his hush money criminal case for Jan. 10 — little over a week before he's due to return to the White House — but indicated he wouldn't be jailed.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL R. SISAK and JENNIFER PELTZ, Associated Press, NewsNation
